preconditions.server: Canary loads the active data and data-otservbr-global datapacks
preconditions.database: Disposable test database or isolated player KV namespace
preconditions.character: Fresh non-GM character unless a scenario states otherwise

steps: Start Canary and load register_achievements.lua.
Read Game.getAchievements().
Compare all registered IDs, names, grades, points and secret flags with the parsed registry.
expected: Every explicit registry entry is registered exactly once.
No explicit high ID after a gap is omitted.
No duplicate ID or duplicate exact name is accepted silently.

steps: Award the highest explicit achievement ID to a fresh character without displaying a message.
Call Player.getAchievements(), Player.getPublicAchievements() or Player.getSecretAchievements() as appropriate.
Request the character achievement list through the protocol-facing path.
expected: The highest explicit achievement is returned despite lower ID gaps.
Enumeration never uses a sparse-table length boundary.

steps: Call Game.isAchievementSecret with one public achievement ID and name.
Call it with one secret achievement ID and name.
Call it with an invalid ID and invalid name.
expected: Public inputs return false and secret inputs return true.
ID and name forms use resolved registry metadata.
Invalid inputs return false and log a bounded error without a secondary Lua failure.

steps: Record initial achievement points.
Award one known achievement.
Award the same achievement again.
Remove it twice.
expected: The first award succeeds and adds exactly the configured points.
The second award is rejected without adding points.
The first removal subtracts exactly the configured points.
The second removal is rejected without underflow.

steps: Award one public and one secret achievement.
Log out and reload the player.
Read unlocked IDs, timestamps, points and secret count.
expected: Both achievements survive reload with stable IDs and timestamps.
The point total equals the sum of the two definitions.
The secret count includes only the secret definition.

steps: Persist an unlocked achievement under its canonical current name.
Evaluate a proposed definition rename in a disposable migration test.
Reload the original and migrated records.
expected: A rename is rejected unless an alias or migration preserves name-keyed unlocked state.
Existing timestamps and points remain stable.
A call-site typo is repaired at the call site rather than by renaming the canonical definition.

steps: Choose an active addAchievementProgress path with a known threshold.
Advance to threshold minus one and reload the player.
Advance once more, then attempt additional increments.
expected: Progress persists across reload.
The achievement is awarded exactly at the threshold.
Additional increments do not duplicate the award or points.

steps: On an isolated GM test character call addAllAchievements(false).
Verify the complete explicit registry including IDs after gaps.
Call removeAllAchievements() and verify points and unlocked state.
expected: Bulk award covers every explicit entry exactly once.
Bulk removal clears every awarded entry and returns points to zero.

steps: Select every reference row whose grade, secrecy, points, ID or exact name conflicts with Canary.
Review the current source revision and the active Canary content that depends on the definition.
Run persistence and point-accounting tests before proposing any change.
expected: No metadata is changed from the wiki alone.
Every accepted change has exact source and runtime evidence.
Name changes include an explicit migration or compatibility alias plan.

steps: Select one reference achievement absent from the Canary registry.
Prove the corresponding quest, item, NPC, event or subsystem exists in the active server version.
Prove a safe award condition, current-player backfill decision and denied-path behavior.
expected: A reference-only row remains conflicting/unresolved until content and an award path are proven.
A definition is not added without the matching gameplay and tests.
Backfill is explicit and idempotent.

steps: Choose one static addAchievement or addAchievementProgress candidate from each handler class.
Validate all prerequisite storage, item, NPC, boss, movement and registration dependencies.
Execute both the qualifying path and a denied/incomplete path.
expected: A static call is promoted from partially-confirmed only after semantic reachability is proven.
The qualifying path awards exactly once.
The denied path does not award early.

steps: Create an existing-player state that already satisfies a newly implemented condition but lacks the achievement.
Load the player at the selected bounded backfill integration point.
Repeat load and normal gameplay evaluation.
expected: The backfill decision is explicit: award, intentionally no backfill, or blocked pending evidence.
Any award occurs exactly once and does not replace an existing timestamp.
Repeated load/evaluation is idempotent.
Each selected trigger awards the exact registry achievement once.
Prerequisites, storage transitions and rewards remain unchanged.
A denied or incomplete path does not award early.

statusPromotionRules.confirmed: Reference condition, definition, active handler, semantic reachability, persistence/backfill and runtime/test evidence are all proven.
statusPromotionRules.partially-confirmed: Definition and a static award/progress candidate exist, but semantic reachability or runtime proof is incomplete.
statusPromotionRules.definition-only: A bounded reviewed subsystem proves the definition exists and no usable active handler is registered in that scope.
statusPromotionRules.handler-missing: A required award handler is specifically proven absent.
statusPromotionRules.unresolved: Evidence is dynamic, indirect, incomplete or not yet reviewed.
statusPromotionRules.conflicting: Reference and Canary identity, metadata or subsystem evidence disagree.
statusPromotionRules.intentionally-unsupported: A versioned, documented support decision with tests explicitly excludes the achievement.

acceptance.staticAudit: No undefined static achievement reference remains.
acceptance.sourceCatalog: Every current listed reference row is represented with revision/hash provenance without copied long prose.
acceptance.runtime: All blocking scenarios for a changed subsystem pass on a disposable character/database.
acceptance.regression: Representative trigger scenarios run in CI or a documented staging gate.
acceptance.auditBoundary: Unresolved and conflicting rows are never promoted without evidence.
